NKJV.1: Paul, a bondservant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s elect and the acknowledgment of the truth which accords with godliness,
NKJV.2: in hope of eternal life which God, who cannot lie, promised before time began,
NKJV.3: but has in due time manifested His word through preaching, which was committed to me according to the commandment of God our Savior;
NKJV.4: To Titus, a true son in our common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
NKJV.5: For this reason I left you in Crete, that you should set in order the things that are lacking, and appoint elders in every city as I commanded you—
NKJV.6: if a man is blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of dissipation or insubordination.
NKJV.7: For a bishop must be blameless, as a steward of God, not self-willed, not quick-tempered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for money,
NKJV.8: but hospitable, a lover of what is good, sober-minded, just, holy, self-controlled,
NKJV.9: holding fast the faithful word as he has been taught, that he may be able, by sound doctrine, both to exhort and convict those who contradict.
NKJV.10: For there are many insubordinate, both idle talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,
NKJV.11: whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole households, teaching things which they ought not, for the sake of dishonest gain.
NKJV.12: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”
NKJV.13: This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,
NKJV.14: not giving heed to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn from the truth.
NKJV.15: To the pure all things are pure, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is pure; but even their mind and conscience are defiled.
NKJV.16: They profess to know God, but in works they deny Him, being abominable, disobedient, and disqualified for every good work.

ESV.1: Paul, a servant  of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the sake of the faith of God's elect and their knowledge of the truth, which accords with godliness,
ESV.2: in hope of eternal life, which God, who never lies, promised before the ages began
ESV.3: and at the proper time manifested in his word  through the preaching with which I have been entrusted by the command of God our Savior;
ESV.4: To Titus, my true child in a common faith: Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
ESV.5: This is why I left you in Crete, so that you might put what remained into order, and appoint elders in every town as I directed you —
ESV.6: if anyone is above reproach, the husband of one wife,  and his children are believers  and not open to the charge of debauchery or insubordination.
ESV.7: For an overseer,  as God's steward, must be above reproach. He must not be arrogant or quick-tempered or a drunkard or violent or greedy for gain,
ESV.8: but hospitable, a lover of good, self-controlled, upright, holy, and disciplined.
ESV.9: He must hold firm to the trustworthy word as taught, so that he may be able to give instruction in sound  doctrine and also to rebuke those who contradict it.
ESV.10: For there are many who are insubordinate, empty talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision party.
ESV.11: They must be silenced, since they are upsetting whole families by teaching for shameful gain what they ought not to teach.
ESV.12: One of the Cretans,  a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”
ESV.13: This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,
ESV.14: not devoting themselves to Jewish myths and the commands of people who turn away from the truth.
ESV.15: To the pure, all things are pure, but to the defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their minds and their consciences are defiled.
ESV.16: They profess to know God, but they deny him by their works. They are detestable, disobedient, unfit for any good work.

NASB.1: Paul, a bond-servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the faith of those chosen of God and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness,
NASB.2: in the hope of eternal life, which God, who cannot lie, promised long ages ago,
NASB.3: but at the proper time manifested, [even] His word, in the proclamation with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior,
NASB.4: To Titus, my true child in a common faith: Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
NASB.5: For this reason I left you in Crete, that you would set in order what remains and appoint elders in every city as I directed you,
NASB.6: [namely], if any man is above reproach, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, not accused of dissipation or rebellion.
NASB.7: For the overseer must be above reproach as God's steward, not self-willed, not quick-tempered, not addicted to wine, not pugnacious, not fond of sordid gain,
NASB.8: but hospitable, loving what is good, sensible, just, devout, self-controlled,
NASB.9: holding fast the faithful word which is in accordance with the teaching, so that he will be able both to exhort in sound doctrine and to refute those who contradict.
NASB.10: For there are many rebellious men, empty talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,
NASB.11: who must be silenced because they are upsetting whole families, teaching things they should not [teach] for the sake of sordid gain.
NASB.12: One of themselves, a prophet of their own, said, 'Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.'
NASB.13: This testimony is true. For this reason reprove them severely so that they may be sound in the faith,
NASB.14: not paying attention to Jewish myths and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
NASB.15: To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure, but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.
NASB.16: They profess to know God, but by [their] deeds they deny [Him], being detestable and disobedient and worthless for any good deed.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I have been sent to proclaim faith to those God has chosen and to teach them to know the truth that shows them how to live godly lives.
NLT.2: This truth gives them confidence that they have eternal life, which God — who does not lie — promised them before the world began.
NLT.3: And now at just the right time he has revealed this message, which we announce to everyone. It is by the command of God our Savior that I have been entrusted with this work for him.
NLT.4: I am writing to Titus, my true son in the faith that we share. May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior give you grace and peace.
NLT.5: I left you on the island of Crete so you could complete our work there and appoint elders in each town as I instructed you.
NLT.6: An elder must live a blameless life. He must be faithful to his wife, and his children must be believers who don’t have a reputation for being wild or rebellious.
NLT.7: A church leader is a manager of God’s household, so he must live a blameless life. He must not be arrogant or quick-tempered; he must not be a heavy drinker, violent, or dishonest with money.
NLT.8: Rather, he must enjoy having guests in his home, and he must love what is good. He must live wisely and be just. He must live a devout and disciplined life.
NLT.9: He must have a strong belief in the trustworthy message he was taught; then he will be able to encourage others with wholesome teaching and show those who oppose it where they are wrong.
NLT.10: For there are many rebellious people who engage in useless talk and deceive others. This is especially true of those who insist on circumcision for salvation.
NLT.11: They must be silenced, because they are turning whole families away from the truth by their false teaching. And they do it only for money.
NLT.12: Even one of their own men, a prophet from Crete, has said about them, “The people of Crete are all liars, cruel animals, and lazy gluttons.”
NLT.13: This is true. So reprimand them sternly to make them strong in the faith.
NLT.14: They must stop listening to Jewish myths and the commands of people who have turned away from the truth.
NLT.15: Everything is pure to those whose hearts are pure. But nothing is pure to those who are corrupt and unbelieving, because their minds and consciences are corrupted.
NLT.16: Such people claim they know God, but they deny him by the way they live. They are detestable and disobedient, worthless for doing anything good.

WEB.1: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s chosen ones, and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness,
WEB.2: in hope of eternal life, which God, who can’t lie, promised before time began;
WEB.3: but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior;
WEB.4: to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
WEB.5: I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking, and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you;
WEB.6: if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior.
WEB.7: For the overseer must be blameless, as God’s steward; not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain;
WEB.8: but given to hospitality, a lover of good, sober minded, fair, holy, self-controlled;
WEB.9: holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him.
WEB.10: For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,
WEB.11: whose mouths must be stopped; men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain’s sake.
WEB.12: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons.”
WEB.13: This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,
WEB.14: not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
WEB.15: To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.
WEB.16: They profess that they know God, but by their deeds they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓔ</sup> a servant of God <sup>ⓕ</sup> and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for <sup>[1]</sup> the faith of God’s elect <sup>ⓖ</sup> and their knowledge of the truth <sup>ⓗ</sup> that leads <sup>[2]</sup> to godliness, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.2: in the hope of eternal life <sup>ⓙ</sup> that God, who cannot lie, <sup>ⓚ</sup> promised before time began. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.3: In his own time he has revealed <sup>ⓜ</sup> his word <sup>ⓝ</sup> in the preaching with which I was entrusted <sup>ⓐ</sup> by the command <sup>ⓑ</sup> of God our Savior: <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: To Titus, <sup>ⓓ</sup> my true son <sup>ⓔ</sup> in our common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.5: The reason I left you in Crete <sup>ⓖ</sup> was to set right what was left undone and, as I directed you, to appoint elders <sup>ⓗ</sup> in every town.
CSB17.6: An elder <sup>ⓘ</sup> must be blameless: <sup>ⓙ</sup> the husband of one wife, with faithful <sup>[3]</sup> children who are not accused of wildness or rebellion.
CSB17.7: As an overseer <sup>ⓚ</sup> of God’s household, he must be blameless: not arrogant, not hot-tempered, not an excessive drinker, not a bully, not greedy for money,
CSB17.8: but hospitable, loving what is good, sensible, righteous, holy, self-controlled,
CSB17.9: holding to the faithful message as taught, <sup>ⓛ</sup> so that he will be able both to encourage with sound teaching <sup>ⓜ</sup> and to refute those who contradict it.
CSB17.10: For there are many rebellious people, full of empty talk and deception, especially those from the circumcision party.
CSB17.11: It is necessary to silence them; they are ruining entire households by teaching what they shouldn’t in order to get money dishonestly.
CSB17.12: One of their very own prophets said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”
CSB17.13: This testimony is true. <sup>ⓝ</sup> For this reason, rebuke them sharply, <sup>ⓐ</sup> so that they may be sound in the faith
CSB17.14: and may not pay attention to Jewish myths <sup>ⓑ</sup> and the commands of people who reject the truth. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.15: To the pure, everything is pure, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is pure; in fact, both their mind and conscience <sup>ⓓ</sup> are defiled.
CSB17.16: They claim to know God, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but they deny him by their works. <sup>ⓕ</sup> They are detestable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Paul, a bond-servant of God and an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Jesus Christ, for the faith of God's chosen ones and [to lead and encourage them to recognize and pursue] the knowledge of the truth which leads to godliness,
AMP.2: based on the hope and divine guarantee of eternal life, [the life] which God, who is ever truthful and without deceit, promised before the ages of time began,
AMP.3: and at the appointed time has made known His word and revealed it as His message, through preaching, which was entrusted to me according to the command of God our Savior—
AMP.4: To Titus, my true child in a common faith: Grace and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
AMP.5: For this reason I left you behind in Crete, so that you would set right what remains unfinished, and appoint elders in every city as I directed you,
AMP.6: namely, a man of unquestionable integrity, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, not accused of being immoral or rebellious.
AMP.7: For the overseer, as God's steward, must be blameless, not self-willed, not quick-tempered, not addicted to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain [but financially ethical].
AMP.8: And he must be hospitable [to believers, as well as strangers], a lover of what is good, sensible (upright), fair, devout, self-disciplined [above reproach—whether in public or in private].
AMP.9: He must hold firmly to the trustworthy word [of God] as it was taught to him, so that he will be able both to give accurate instruction in sound [reliable, error-free] doctrine and to refute those who contradict [it by explaining their error].
AMP.10: For there are many rebellious men who are empty talkers [just windbags] and deceivers; especially those of the circumcision [those Jews who insist that Gentile believers must be circumcised and keep the Law in order to be saved].
AMP.11: They must be silenced, because they are upsetting whole families by teaching things they should not teach for the purpose of dishonest financial gain.
AMP.12: One of them [Epimenides, a Cretan], a prophet of their own, said, "Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons."
AMP.13: This description is true. So rebuke them sharply so that they will be sound in the faith and free from doctrinal error,
AMP.14: not paying attention to Jewish myths and the commandments and rules of men who turn their backs on the truth.
AMP.15: To the pure, all things are pure; but to the corrupt and unbelieving, nothing is pure; both their mind and their conscience are corrupted.
AMP.16: They profess to know God [to recognize and be acquainted with Him], but by their actions they deny and disown Him. They are detestable and disobedient and worthless for good work of any kind.

MSG.1: I, Paul, am God's slave and Christ's agent for promoting the faith among God's chosen people, getting out the accurate word on God and how to respond rightly to it.
MSG.2: My aim is to raise hopes by pointing the way to life without end. This is the life God promised long ago—and he doesn't break promises!
MSG.3: And then when the time was ripe, he went public with his truth. I've been entrusted to proclaim this Message by order of our Savior, God himself.
MSG.4: Dear Titus, legitimate son in the faith: Receive everything God our Father and Jesus our Savior give you!
MSG.5: I left you in charge in Crete so you could complete what I left half-done. Appoint leaders in every town according to my instructions.
MSG.6: As you select them, ask, "Is this man well-thought-of? Are his children believers? Do they respect him and stay out of trouble?"
MSG.7: It's important that a church leader, responsible for the affairs in God's house, be looked up to—not pushy, not short-tempered, not a drunk, not a bully, not money-hungry.
MSG.8: He must welcome people, be helpful, wise, fair, reverent, have a good grip on himself,
MSG.9: and have a good grip on the Message, knowing how to use the truth to either spur people on in knowledge or stop them in their tracks if they oppose it.
MSG.10: For there are a lot of rebels out there, full of loose, confusing, and deceiving talk. Those who were brought up religious and ought to know better are the worst.
MSG.11: They've got to be shut up. They're disrupting entire families with their teaching, and all for the sake of a fast buck.
MSG.12: One of their own prophets said it best: The Cretans are liars from the womb, barking dogs, lazy bellies.
MSG.13: He certainly spoke the truth. Get on them right away. Stop that diseased talk of Jewish make-believe and made-up rules
MSG.14: so they can recover a robust faith.
MSG.15: Everything is clean to the clean-minded; nothing is clean to dirty-minded unbelievers. They leave their dirty fingerprints on every thought and act.
MSG.16: They say they know God, but their actions speak louder than their words. They're real creeps, disobedient good-for-nothings.

NIV.1: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ for the faith of God’s elect and the knowledge of the truth that leads to godliness—
NIV.2: a faith and knowledge resting on the hope of eternal life, which God, who does not lie, promised before the beginning of time,
NIV.3: and at his appointed season he brought his word to light through the preaching entrusted to me by the command of God our Savior,
NIV.4:
To Titus, my true son in our common faith:
Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
NIV.5:
Titus’s Task on Crete
The reason I left you in Crete was that you might straighten out what was left unfinished and appoint elders in every town, as I directed you.
NIV.6: An elder must be blameless, the husband of but one wife, a man whose children believe and are not open to the charge of being wild and disobedient.
NIV.7: Since an overseer is entrusted with God’s work, he must be blameless—not overbearing, not quick-tempered, not given to drunkenness, not violent, not pursuing dishonest gain.
NIV.8: Rather he must be hospitable, one who loves what is good, who is self-controlled, upright, holy and disciplined.
NIV.9: He must hold firmly to the trustworthy message as it has been taught, so that he can encourage others by sound doctrine and refute those who oppose it.
NIV.10: For there are many rebellious people, mere talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision group.
NIV.11: They must be silenced, because they are ruining whole households by teaching things they ought not to teach—and that for the sake of dishonest gain.
NIV.12: Even one of their own prophets has said, “Cretans are always liars, evil brutes, lazy gluttons.”
NIV.13: This testimony is true. Therefore, rebuke them sharply, so that they will be sound in the faith
NIV.14: and will pay no attention to Jewish myths or to the commands of those who reject the truth.
NIV.15: To the pure, all things are pure, but to those who are corrupted and do not believe, nothing is pure. In fact, both their minds and consciences are corrupted.
NIV.16: They claim to know God, but by their actions they deny him. They are detestable, disobedient and unfit for doing anything good.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the sake of the faith of God’s elect and the knowledge of the truth that is in accordance with godliness,
NRSVCE.2: in the hope of eternal life that God, who never lies, promised before the ages began —
NRSVCE.3: in due time he revealed his word through the proclamation with which I have been entrusted by the command of God our Savior,
NRSVCE.4: To Titus, my loyal child in the faith we share: Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
NRSVCE.5: I left you behind in Crete for this reason, so that you should put in order what remained to be done, and should appoint elders in every town, as I directed you:
NRSVCE.6: someone who is blameless, married only once, whose children are believers, not accused of debauchery and not rebellious.
NRSVCE.7: For a bishop, as God’s steward, must be blameless; he must not be arrogant or quick-tempered or addicted to wine or violent or greedy for gain;
NRSVCE.8: but he must be hospitable, a lover of goodness, prudent, upright, devout, and self-controlled.
NRSVCE.9: He must have a firm grasp of the word that is trustworthy in accordance with the teaching, so that he may be able both to preach with sound doctrine and to refute those who contradict it.
NRSVCE.10: There are also many rebellious people, idle talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision;
NRSVCE.11: they must be silenced, since they are upsetting whole families by teaching for sordid gain what it is not right to teach.
NRSVCE.12: It was one of them, their very own prophet, who said, “Cretans are always liars, vicious brutes, lazy gluttons.”
NRSVCE.13: That testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply, so that they may become sound in the faith,
NRSVCE.14: not paying attention to Jewish myths or to commandments of those who reject the truth.
NRSVCE.15: To the pure all things are pure, but to the corrupt and unbelieving nothing is pure. Their very minds and consciences are corrupted.
NRSVCE.16: They profess to know God, but they deny him by their actions. They are detestable, disobedient, unfit for any good work.

NET.1: From Paul, a slave of God and apostle of Jesus Christ, to further the faith of God’s chosen ones and the knowledge of the truth that is in keeping with godliness,
NET.2: in hope of eternal life, which God, who does not lie, promised before the ages began.
NET.3: But now in his own time he has made his message evident through the preaching I was entrusted with according to the command of God our Savior.
NET.4: To Titus, my genuine son in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior!
NET.5: The reason I left you in Crete was to set in order the remaining matters and to appoint elders in every town, as I directed you.
NET.6: An elder must be blameless, the husband of one wife, with faithful children who cannot be charged with dissipation or rebellion.
NET.7: For the overseer must be blameless as one entrusted with God’s work, not arrogant, not prone to anger, not a drunkard, not violent, not greedy for gain.
NET.8: Instead he must be hospitable, devoted to what is good, sensible, upright, devout, and self-controlled.
NET.9: He must hold firmly to the faithful message as it has been taught, so that he will be able to give exhortation in such healthy teaching and correct those who speak against it.
NET.10: For there are many rebellious people, idle talkers, and deceivers, especially those with Jewish connections,
NET.11: who must be silenced because they mislead whole families by teaching for dishonest gain what ought not to be taught.
NET.12: A certain one of them, in fact, one of their own prophets, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”
NET.13: Such testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply that they may be healthy in the faith
NET.14: and not pay attention to Jewish myths and commands of people who reject the truth.
NET.15: All is pure to those who are pure. But to those who are corrupt and unbelieving, nothing is pure, but both their minds and consciences are corrupted.
NET.16: They profess to know God but with their deeds they deny him, since they are detestable, disobedient, and unfit for any good deed.
